name: Perplexity AI
purpose: AI-powered search that cites sources. Replaces Google for any research-heavy query. Pro unlocks GPT-4o, Claude, and deep research mode.

name: Gamma.app
purpose: AI-generated slide decks, documents, and webpages in under 2 minutes. Paste an outline → full presentation with design. Best for rapid decks you'd otherwise spend hours in Google Slides.

name: Lovable.dev
purpose: AI-generated full-stack apps from a description. React + Supabase + Tailwind + shadcn/ui. Deploy to lovable.dev subdomain instantly. Best for: MVPs, hackathons, client demos.

name: NotebookLM
purpose: Google's AI notebook that reads your sources and answers questions based only on them. Best feature: generates a podcast-style audio overview of any document set. Zero hallucination.

name: Cursor IDE
purpose: The best AI code editor. VS Code fork with Claude/GPT-4 built in. Tab completion that understands your whole codebase. Agent mode for multi-file changes. Use this for serious projects.

name: Windsurf (Codeium)
purpose: Cursor's main competitor from Codeium. Cascade AI agent has deeper multi-file awareness. Better free tier than Cursor. Use when Cursor hits its limits.

name: Mobbin
purpose: 50,000+ real mobile app screens organized by pattern, industry, and element. The best UI reference for mobile design. Screenshot → paste into V0/Lovable as reference.

name: ReactBits
purpose: Library of animated, interactive React components you copy-paste into projects. Particles, text effects, card animations, backgrounds. Elevates UI from bland to impressive instantly.

name: Wispr Flow
purpose: Dictate in any text field on Mac. Press hotkey → speak → AI transcribes + polishes in real-time. Faster than typing for anything over 50 words. Works everywhere: Cursor, Claude, email, Notion.

name: Google Whisk → ezgif Pipeline
purpose: Blend 3 reference images into a new AI-generated image. Subject + Scene + Style → output. Then animate with ezgif. Portfolio-grade visuals in under 5 minutes.

name: Manus AI
purpose: General-purpose autonomous AI agent that browses the web, writes code, and completes multi-step tasks end-to-end. Give it a task and walk away.

name: Perplexity Deep Research
purpose: Perplexity's extended research mode. Searches 20+ sources, synthesizes into a 2000+ word structured report with full citations. Takes 2-3 min. Replaces hours of manual research.

name: 21st.dev Magic MCP
purpose: MCP server that lets Claude search and use UI components from 21st.dev's library. Say "add a pricing table" → Claude fetches the exact component and inserts it. Design system integration built in.

name: Sprout / Jobsuit
purpose: AI tools for job searching: resume tailoring, application tracking, cover letter gen. Useful during active job search but not day-to-day. Try when actively applying.

name: Antigravity (Atoms IDE)
purpose: The build system behind Atoms-style interactive portfolios. 3-phase approach: Phase 1 (scroll canvas), Phase 2 (video scrub), Phase 3 (mouse scrub + combined). Canvas + GSAP based.

name: Bolt.new
purpose: StackBlitz's AI builder that runs Node.js in the browser (WebContainers). Generates full-stack apps, runs npm, installs packages, hot-reloads — all in a tab. No setup required.

name: V0 by Vercel
purpose: Vercel's AI component generator. Describe or screenshot → get production React components with shadcn/ui + Tailwind. Copy component code directly into your project. Best UI generation tool available.

name: Google Stitch (DESIGN.md)
purpose: Google's AI design tool that generates UI from descriptions + creates DESIGN.md files — a markdown design system description that Claude can read to maintain visual consistency across generated code.

name: Firebase Studio
purpose: Google's full-stack AI development environment. Gemini-powered code generation + Firebase backend + cloud preview. Good for apps that need Firebase/Firestore.

name: Figma → Code (Dev Mode)
purpose: Figma's Dev Mode exports CSS, React components, and design tokens directly from designs. Use when working from a designer's Figma file. Saves hours of manual CSS translation.

name: Replit
purpose: Browser-based IDE with AI assistant, instant deployment, and multiplayer editing. Best for: learning environments, collaboration, quick Python/Node scripts without local setup.

name: FlutterFlow
purpose: Visual Flutter app builder. Drag-and-drop UI + logic, then export real Flutter/Dart code. If you need native mobile (iOS + Android) without deep Flutter knowledge, this is your tool.

name: Chrome Extension Builder
purpose: AI tools and templates for building Chrome extensions with Manifest V3. Faster scaffold than starting from scratch. Good for adding browser automation and productivity tools.

name: Pre-Launch Production Checklist
purpose: Everything to verify before launching any app. Security, performance, SEO, auth, error handling. Save this. Run it before every deploy that goes public.

name: Error Lens
purpose: Shows TypeScript/ESLint errors and warnings inline on the affected line — no more clicking to the Problems panel. Makes debugging 2x faster by keeping errors visible at all times.

name: Prettier + ESLint
purpose: Prettier auto-formats code on save. ESLint catches logic errors and style violations. Together they enforce consistent, clean code automatically — you never manually format again.

name: GitLens
purpose: Adds git blame inline — hover any line to see who wrote it, when, and in which commit. File history, branch comparison, interactive rebase UI. Makes git visible without leaving the editor.

name: Live Server
purpose: One-click local dev server for static HTML/CSS/JS files with automatic hot-reload. Right-click any HTML file → "Open with Live Server" → instant browser preview that refreshes on save.

name: Thunder Client
purpose: Postman-equivalent API testing built into VS Code. Test REST/GraphQL endpoints without leaving the editor. Lightweight, fast, stores collections in your project. Free.

name: Path Intellisense
purpose: Autocompletes file paths as you type in import statements and src attributes. Eliminates path typos and "module not found" errors. Tiny extension, massive daily time saving.

name: Console Ninja
purpose: Shows console.log() output directly next to the line that logged it — no more Alt-Tab to browser DevTools to check logs. Runtime values appear inline in the editor.

name: Codeium
purpose: Free GitHub Copilot alternative with AI code completion. No usage limits on free tier. Use when Cursor's completions run out or as primary autocomplete in standard VS Code.

name: Indent Rainbow + Better Comments
purpose: Indent Rainbow: colorizes indentation levels so nested code is instantly readable. Better Comments: color-codes comment types (TODO, FIXME, !, ?) for visual scanning.

name: Auto Import + Tailwind IntelliSense
purpose: Auto Import: automatically adds import statements when you use components/functions. Tailwind IntelliSense: autocompletes Tailwind class names with a preview of what they do.

name: DotENV + ENV File Syntax
purpose: Syntax highlighting for .env files. Makes managing environment variables readable. Pairs with dotenv-vault or 1Password for secret sharing across teams.

name: Todo Tree
purpose: Finds all TODO/FIXME/HACK/NOTE comments across your entire codebase and shows them in a sidebar tree. Useful for solo projects to track in-code tasks without a separate tool.

name: Claude Projects — 3-File System
purpose: Every serious project needs 3 files in its Claude Project: CLAUDE.md (context), FEEDBACK.md (session learnings), and a SCRATCH.md (working notes). This creates persistent AI memory across sessions.

name: CLAUDE.md Mastery
purpose: The single most important file in any Claude Code project. Andrej Karpathy reported 65%→94% accuracy improvement after writing a proper CLAUDE.md. Every project needs one.

name: Senior Collaborator — System Prompt
purpose: Makes Claude act as a senior engineer who pushes back, flags issues proactively, and refuses to leave TODOs. Use this for serious coding sessions.

name: LEAN ENGINE Protocol
purpose: A prompt protocol that reduces Claude's output by 60-70% without losing quality. Eliminates preamble, hedging, repetition, and filler. Claude writes like a senior engineer, not a chatbot.

name: LLM Council
purpose: A prompt that makes Claude simulate 3-5 distinct AI personas, each with different reasoning styles, debate a problem, and synthesize the best answer. Gets significantly better answers on complex decisions.

name: Wiki-Brain (Obsidian + Claude)
purpose: A Claude Code skill that turns Obsidian into a compound AI memory system. Claude reads your notes, builds connections, maintains a BRAIN.md index, and grows smarter about your knowledge base over time.

name: Headroom — Multi-Agent Framework
purpose: Multi-agent orchestration library built on Anthropic's Agent SDK. Route tasks to specialized agents, coordinate parallel work, build compound AI systems. The framework for serious AI engineering.

name: Session Start / End Rituals
purpose: Structured prompts for beginning and ending Claude sessions. Session Start loads context correctly; Session End extracts learnings for FEEDBACK.md. Makes every session compounding.

name: 10 Thinking Partner Prompts
purpose: 10 prompts that transform Claude from task executor to strategic thought partner. For decisions, planning, and getting unstuck.

name: Life OS — Claude as Life System
purpose: Using Claude Projects as a personal operating system: goal tracking, weekly reviews, decision journaling, habit accountability. The Claude version of a Notion Life OS.

name: Playwright MCP
purpose: Give Claude a real browser. Navigate pages, click elements, fill forms, take screenshots, scrape data. Claude operates the browser as you would. Best MCP for web automation tasks.

name: Context7 MCP
purpose: Gives Claude access to live, version-specific documentation for 3000+ libraries. Instead of hallucinating outdated APIs, Claude fetches the actual current docs. Essential for any framework work.

name: Supabase MCP
purpose: Claude talks directly to your Supabase database. Run queries, create tables, write migrations, manage RLS policies — all via natural language. Eliminates most Supabase dashboard time.

name: GitHub MCP
purpose: Claude talks to GitHub. Search repos, read code, create issues, review PRs, manage branches — all via natural language. Turns Claude into your GitHub power user.

name: BlenderMCP
purpose: Claude controls Blender via MCP socket server. Create 3D objects, scenes, materials, and animations through natural language. No Blender expertise needed.

name: Notion MCP
purpose: Claude reads and writes to Notion. Search pages, create notes, update databases, add task entries — without opening Notion. Great for saving Claude outputs directly to your workspace.

name: Filesystem MCP
purpose: Claude reads, writes, and organizes files on your computer. Mass rename, restructure directories, process file contents, generate files from templates — all from natural language.

name: Email + Calendar MCP
purpose: Claude reads email threads, drafts replies, schedules events, and manages your calendar. Turns Claude into a real executive assistant for communications.

name: Exa Search MCP
purpose: Neural search API that finds semantically relevant content, not just keyword matches. Claude uses Exa to research topics with current, high-quality sources. Better than Bing for research queries.

name: Linear MCP
purpose: Claude manages your Linear workspace. Create issues, update statuses, triage bugs, plan sprints — from natural language. Turns Claude into your project management assistant.

name: Slack MCP
purpose: Claude reads Slack messages, searches channels, drafts responses, and posts updates. Turns Claude into a Slack power user for message summaries and team communication automation.

name: Memory MCP
purpose: Gives Claude a persistent knowledge graph that survives across sessions. Store facts, preferences, project context — Claude remembers them in all future conversations.

name: MCP Config Reference
purpose: Reference for the Claude Desktop config file that wires up all MCP servers. Location, format, common patterns, and debugging tips.

name: 21st.dev Magic MCP
purpose: Claude searches 21st.dev's UI component library and inserts components directly into your code. Say "add a dark mode toggle" → Claude finds + installs the exact component. Design system aware.

name: Obsidian Mind
purpose: Gives Claude Code a persistent brain via your Obsidian vault. Hooks fire on every session start — Claude reads your North Star, active projects, open tasks, and recent changes before you type a word.

name: kepano/obsidian-skills
purpose: Official Obsidian agent skills by Kepano (Obsidian creator). 5 skills that teach Claude Code everything about Obsidian: markdown syntax, CLI commands, Bases (database views), JSON Canvas, and web extraction via Defuddle.

name: Obsidian Second Brain
purpose: Your vault rewrites itself. Extends Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern: new sources update existing pages instead of appending new ones. 33 commands, 4 scheduled agents, live research from X + web + YouTube. The vault gets smarter while you sleep.

name: LLM Council Skill
purpose: Turns one question into five independent expert opinions + peer review + a chairman's verdict. Based on Karpathy's LLM Council methodology. For decisions where you need more than one perspective.

name: Taste-Skill — Anti-Slop Framework
purpose: 17.5k⭐ GitHub. A suite of 9 Claude Code skills that prevent generic AI-generated UI. Enforces aesthetic taste through tunable dials, typography rules, spacing systems, and color theory built into the model's instructions.

name: Awesome DESIGN.md
purpose: 94.9k⭐ GitHub. A collection of 73+ DESIGN.md files for major tech brands. Drop any file in your project and Claude generates UI that matches that brand's exact aesthetic — Apple, Notion, Linear, Stripe, Vercel, etc.

name: SkillUI / npx skillui
purpose: CLI that reverse-engineers any website's design system into a DESIGN.md file. Point at any URL → get a complete design system description Claude can use. Turn any website's aesthetic into a Claude skill.

name: UI UX Pro Max Skill
purpose: A massive Claude skill containing 161 UI/UX reasoning rules, 67 named UI styles (glassmorphism, brutalism, etc.), 161 color palettes with hex values, and a BM25 ranking engine to match your prompt to the best style.

name: Impeccable
purpose: 43.6k⭐ GitHub. 23 design commands + 45 anti-pattern detection rules for Claude Code. Catches: inconsistent spacing, low-contrast text, accessibility violations, visual clutter. Makes AI-generated UI professional-grade.

name: Wiki-Brain Skill
purpose: Claude Code skill that turns Obsidian into a compound AI memory system. Claude indexes your vault, maintains a BRAIN.md knowledge graph, and recalls relevant notes in any conversation. Persistent AI memory.

name: 9router — Multi-Agent Router
purpose: A Claude Code skill implementing 9-way agent routing. Routes tasks to specialized Claude agents based on intent classification. Efficient for complex multi-domain tasks.

name: Agent-Browser Skill
purpose: Claude Code skill that gives Claude structured browser access for research tasks. Implements search → read → synthesize loops. Claude researches topics autonomously and returns sourced summaries.

name: MCP-Builder Skill
purpose: Claude Code skill that helps you build custom MCP servers. Scaffolds the boilerplate, implements tools, handles the MCP protocol — you describe what you want Claude to be able to do, it builds the server.

name: Web Design Guidelines Skill
purpose: A comprehensive set of web design best practices encoded as a Claude skill. WCAG accessibility rules, responsive design patterns, typography standards — always applied when generating UI.

name: Find-Skills CLI
purpose: A CLI tool for discovering and installing Claude Code skills from the community ecosystem. Search by capability, install in one command. Keeps your Claude Code setup current with new community skills.

name: /critique — Unsparing Review
purpose: Activates brutal honesty mode. Claude finds what's weak, confusing, or missing — with specific fixes, no padding. Use on: landing pages, essays, pitch decks, code, product ideas.

name: /speed-build — Ship Fast
purpose: Build it now. No clarifying questions, no option lists, no confirming. Claude makes the obvious choices and produces complete, runnable code immediately. For when you want velocity over perfect alignment.

name: /architect — System Design Mode
purpose: Activates systems architect mode. Claude designs before building: components, data flow, key decisions with rationale, failure modes, scale considerations. Use for any non-trivial system.

name: /security-audit — Vulnerability Scan
purpose: Full security review mode. Claude looks for: XSS, SQL injection, SSRF, auth gaps, exposed secrets, missing validation. Categorizes by severity. Essential before any public launch.

name: /test-cases — Complete Test Suite Gen
purpose: Generates a complete test suite — happy path, edge cases, error cases, boundary conditions, and things you forgot to think about. Uses Vitest/Jest syntax. TDD accelerator.

name: /refactor — Code Cleanup Mode
purpose: Targeted refactor mode. Priority order: readability → simplicity → performance. Shows before/after with only non-obvious changes explained. For code that works but is painful to read.

name: /pr-description — Perfect PR Copy
purpose: Generates a professional PR description from your diff or change summary. What changed + why + what to test + deployment notes. Makes your PRs look like a senior engineer wrote them.

name: /pre-mortem — Failure Analysis
purpose: Imagine 6 months in the future — the project failed. What went wrong? Pre-mortem forces you to surface risks before they happen, when you can still prevent them.

name: /rubber-duck — Thinking Partner Mode
purpose: Claude asks, you answer. The rubber duck debugging technique at scale — but Claude asks probing questions that expose gaps in your reasoning. For when you need to think, not just get an answer.

name: /performance-audit — Speed Analysis
purpose: Performance review mode: N+1 queries, unnecessary re-renders, missing caching, expensive loops, sync-when-should-be-async. Estimated impact per issue. For when the app is slow and you don't know why.

name: RAG + Hybrid Search Pipeline
purpose: Production-grade Retrieval Augmented Generation with hybrid search: dense vector (ChromaDB) + sparse BM25 keyword + RRF fusion ranking. The real RAG architecture, not toy implementations.

name: Multi-Agent Workflow Pipeline
purpose: Build a production multi-agent system using LangGraph or Anthropic Agent SDK. Orchestrator routes to specialist agents (research, write, review). Demonstrates real AI engineering skills.

name: Interactive Portfolio (Atoms-Style)
purpose: The Atoms-style interactive portfolio with scroll-driven animation, mouse scrub effects, and video sequences. The portfolio that makes recruiters and clients stop and pay attention.

name: Leadify — AI Lead Generation SaaS
purpose: AI-powered B2B lead generation platform. Companies define their ICP, AI finds matching leads from public data sources, scores them, and drafts personalized outreach. Full SaaS with auth + payments.

name: Freshify — Smoothie Bowl App
purpose: Beautiful single-page smoothie bowl app. AI-powered recipe generation, nutrition info, ingredient tracker. Built as a single HTML file — proof that beautiful apps don't need complex stacks.

name: Surf Ranch — Booking Platform
purpose: Premium surf camp booking platform with wave simulation canvas animation, real-time availability, and seamless checkout. Combines beautiful frontend with full booking system backend.

name: Jarvis — Personal AI System
purpose: A personal AI operating system with 6 specialized domains: Finance (portfolio tracking), Health (fitness, nutrition), Learning (spaced repetition), Career (goals, resume), Research (knowledge base), Daily Ops (planning, habits).

name: LoRA Fine-Tuning Project
purpose: Fine-tune a language model on your own data using LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation). Much cheaper than full fine-tuning. Deploy on HuggingFace Spaces. Strong ML portfolio project.

name: Chrome Extension — Productivity Tool
purpose: Build a Chrome extension that adds AI capabilities to any website: summarize pages, highlight key concepts, save to Notion, generate email replies. Highly distributable portfolio piece.

name: Personal Analytics Dashboard
purpose: A self-tracking dashboard pulling from: GitHub (contributions), Spotify (listening habits), fitness tracker, finances, habits, productivity apps. AI generates weekly insights and identifies patterns.

name: AI Study Buddy — Spaced Repetition
purpose: An AI-powered flashcard and spaced repetition system. Auto-generates cards from notes/textbooks, tracks performance with SM-2, surfaces due cards, and explains concepts in new ways when you struggle.

name: ECC — Elliptic Curve Cryptography
purpose: Implement Elliptic Curve Cryptography from scratch in Python. ECDH key exchange, ECDSA signing, secp256k1 curve. Demonstrates deep CS fundamentals and mathematical rigor.

name: Castimedia Portfolio (Antigravity 3-Phase)
purpose: Agency-style portfolio using the full Antigravity/Atoms 3-phase interactive build. Project showcases with scroll animation, case study format, and client work presentation.

name: Discord/Telegram Bot with Claude
purpose: Build a community bot powered by Claude. Answer questions from your server's knowledge base, moderate content, generate content, run polls. Practical and highly distributable.

name: HuggingFace Portfolio Trick
purpose: Deploy ML project demos on HuggingFace Spaces for free. GPU-backed demos that let recruiters interact with your ML work directly. Gradio or Streamlit UI in minutes.

name: AI Fitness Coach
purpose: Upload meal photos → calorie estimation. Workout planner, form analysis via camera, daily habit tracking. Perfect first AI + Vision project — impressive output, manageable complexity.

name: AI Second Brain for Devs
purpose: Remembers conversations, stores project context, lets you search old decisions. "What did we decide last week?" — actually answered. The developer tool version of the Obsidian second brain.

name: Personalized AI News Agent
purpose: Reads hundreds of articles, learns your interests, sends a daily digest with explanations of why each story matters. Agentic workflows, scheduling, and email automation in one project.

name: Open Source Agent Builder
purpose: Visual agent builder. Swap LLMs, configure tools, manage memory and prompt playground, deploy with one click. The hardest project on the list — also the most impressive for a portfolio or productization.

name: AI Job Hunter
purpose: Resume optimizer, LinkedIn profile review, portfolio feedback, job matching, auto-generated cover letters, interview prep. Full AI-powered job application stack — builds portfolio while solving a real problem.

name: SE → AI Engineer: 15 Projects
purpose: 15 projects that solve real production AI problems. None of these are "I called an API and deployed a chatbot." Every one builds a real skill AI teams actually need at scale.

name: 10 Startup Ideas for 2026
purpose: 10 startups that don't exist yet but will by 2026. Each one is a real gap — not "AI version of X," but fundamentally new products enabled by where AI actually is right now.

name: Jarvis System Architecture
purpose: The complete Jarvis architecture: 6 specialized AI domains (Finance, Health, Learning, Career, Research, Daily Ops), central orchestrator, shared memory layer, and multi-channel delivery (Slack, email, dashboard).

name: Finance Intelligence Module
purpose: Jarvis Finance: automated net worth tracking, spending categorization, investment analysis, and monthly financial review with AI insights. Your personal CFO.

name: Learning Accelerator Module
purpose: Jarvis Learning: SM-2 spaced repetition system for any subject, AI-generated cards from your notes, reading tracker, and daily review sessions surfaced in your morning brief.

name: Daily Operations Module
purpose: Jarvis Daily Ops: automated morning brief at 7am (Slack), habit tracking, daily planning session, and evening review. The infrastructure for consistent daily routines.

name: Jarvis Dashboard (Next.js)
purpose: The central Jarvis web dashboard. Domain overview cards, habit streaks, net worth chart, due cards, calendar today, and a central command input. Everything visible at a glance.

name: Voice Interface Module
purpose: Add voice input/output to Jarvis. Speak commands → Whisper transcribes → Claude responds → TTS reads the answer aloud. Hands-free Jarvis for morning routines.

name: Instagram Analytics Module
purpose: Jarvis tracks your Instagram metrics via the Graph API, identifies your best-performing content types, and generates data-driven content strategy recommendations.

name: Inbox Management Module
purpose: Jarvis triages your email and Slack daily. Morning summary: what needs a response, what can wait, what to unsubscribe from. Draft replies for complex threads.

name: Career Development Module
purpose: Jarvis Career: track job applications, maintain resume versions, identify skill gaps, log networking contacts, and generate targeted outreach. Your career strategy co-pilot.

name: Health & Fitness Module
purpose: Jarvis Health: workout logging with progressive overload tracking, nutrition macro tracking, sleep analysis, and weekly health score. AI coaches you toward your fitness goals.

name: Jarvis Agent Routines
purpose: The scheduled automation routines that make Jarvis run on autopilot. Cron schedules, Vercel serverless functions, and multi-step agent workflows that run without you triggering them.

name: Jarvis Tech Stack Reference
purpose: Complete Jarvis technology reference: all dependencies, environment variables, MCP connections, API keys needed, and the full package.json. Save for project setup.

name: HuggingFace ML Courses
purpose: HuggingFace's free courses are the best practical ML education available. NLP course (transformers from scratch), Diffusion Models, Deep RL, Audio — all hands-on with real code.

name: LangChain / LangGraph Academy
purpose: LangChain's official courses on building production LLM applications: RAG systems, agents, LangGraph for multi-agent workflows. Free and directly from the framework builders.

name: fast.ai — Practical Deep Learning
purpose: fast.ai's "Practical Deep Learning for Coders" — the best ML course for people who want to build, not just theorize. Top-down approach: build first, understand why it works second. Completely free.

name: AWS / GCP Free Tier + Certs
purpose: AWS Cloud Practitioner or GCP Associate certification. Validates cloud fundamentals. Both have free practice exams. The cert isn't the goal — understanding cloud infrastructure is.

name: Stanford CS231n + CS224n
purpose: Stanford's graduate ML courses, freely available online. CS231n (Computer Vision) and CS224n (NLP) are the gold standard for understanding deep learning theory at a rigorous level.

name: Kaggle Competitions + Courses
purpose: Kaggle has free micro-courses (Python, ML, SQL, Deep Learning) AND real competitions where you can win medals. The learning path: courses → Getting Started competitions → Featured competitions.

name: Meta React / Next.js Cert (Coursera)
purpose: Meta's React Developer certification on Coursera. Official React cert from the creators of React. Free audit, paid cert (~$50). Best for: students who want a verifiable frontend credential.

name: Google Analytics + Data Studio
purpose: Google Analytics 4 (GA4) + Looker Studio (formerly Data Studio) for tracking and visualizing web/app analytics. Free cert from Google. Useful for every app you launch.

name: Google AI Essentials
purpose: The #1 AI cert employers screen for. Under 2 hours. Free. Covers AI fundamentals, prompt engineering, responsible AI. Goes straight on LinkedIn and is recognized by essentially every major employer.

name: HubSpot Content Marketing
purpose: HubSpot's content marketing certification. Recognized universally in marketing, SaaS, and agency hiring. Proves you understand content strategy, SEO content, lead generation, and results-driven content. Free.

name: Semrush SEO Certification
purpose: Taught by Brian Dean (Backlinko founder — literally the best SEO teacher alive). Under 1 hour. Covers keyword research, on-page SEO, link building strategy. Certificate goes straight to LinkedIn.

name: Claude Certified Architect
purpose: Deloitte pays employees thousands of dollars for this. Proctored (webcam on), legitimately hard. Free for the first few thousand seats. Proves advanced Claude architecture knowledge — multi-agent systems, MCP, production AI design.

name: Anthropic Documentation
purpose: Primary reference for everything Claude. Prompt engineering guide, API reference, tool use, extended thinking, agent SDK. Read the prompt engineering section once properly.

name: GitHub Trending (Daily)
purpose: github.com/trending — daily list of fastest-rising repos. Filter by language. How you discover tools like impeccable, taste-skill, blender-mcp before everyone else. Check once a day.

name: shadcn/ui Component Library
purpose: Copy-paste React components built on Radix UI primitives. Production-quality, accessible, customizable. What V0 generates. The foundation of any serious Next.js project.

name: Andrej Karpathy — YouTube & Writing
purpose: Best ML educator alive. "Neural Networks: Zero to Hero" builds GPT from scratch. Watching this changes how you think about language models. Non-negotiable if you want to understand AI deeply.

name: model context protocol (MCP) Registry
purpose: modelcontextprotocol.io/servers — the official and community registry of MCP servers. 200+ servers covering every API and service. Check here before building a custom MCP.

name: Design Inspiration Sites
purpose: Curated UI/UX inspiration. Use before starting any project: 20 minutes of intentional reference browsing → specific patterns to implement → V0 or Claude code that matches your vision.

name: Hacker News (YC / Show HN)
purpose: news.ycombinator.com — the best signal-to-noise tech news source. "Show HN" posts are developers launching things. The comments are where real engineers discuss tools, disagree with hype, and share production experience.

name: Awesome Lists — Curated GitHub Collections
purpose: GitHub's "Awesome" lists are curated collections of the best resources in any domain. awesome-mcp-servers, awesome-claude-prompts, awesome-llm-apps — bookmarked goldmines for your stack.

name: Vercel AI SDK
purpose: Vercel's SDK for integrating AI into web apps. Unified interface for Claude, OpenAI, Gemini. React hooks for streaming chat. The fastest way to add AI to a Next.js app.

name: Boris Cherny CLAUDE.md Template
purpose: The best public CLAUDE.md template. Adapt it for every project. Establishes conventions, constraints, and workflow instructions that dramatically improve Claude Code output quality.

name: TanStack Libraries
purpose: TanStack's suite: React Query (server state), Table (headless tables), Router (type-safe routing), Form (form state). The gold standard for production React data management.

name: Zustand — Lightweight State Management
purpose: The right state management library for 2025. No boilerplate, no providers, no reducers. Just a store with a getter and setter. Use for global client state that isn't server data.

name: Plausible + Sentry — Monitoring Stack
purpose: Plausible (privacy-first analytics, no cookies) + Sentry (error tracking with full stack traces). The production monitoring stack every launched app needs, set up in 30 minutes.

name: Framer Motion — Animation Library
purpose: Production-grade React animation library. Page transitions, element reveals, drag gestures, spring physics. Used by Vercel, Linear, Raycast. The animation library for serious React projects.

name: Skiper UI
purpose: Premium animated UI component library. Drop-in animated cards, buttons, heroes, navbars — all with Tailwind + Framer Motion. No design system needed.

name: 10x App
purpose: All-in-one app for iOS developers. App Store submission helpers, ASO tools, screenshot generators, review management, and more — designed for indie devs.

name: Layers.to
purpose: The best website design gallery on the internet. Curated, tagged, and searchable. Every time you need inspiration, check Layers first before opening Dribbble or Behance.

name: Animista
purpose: Visual CSS animation playground. See animations live, tweak timing/easing/direction visually, copy the exact CSS. No more guessing keyframe values.

name: Phosphor Icons
purpose: The most flexible icon library for vibe coders. 1,000+ icons in 6 weights — thin, light, regular, bold, fill, duotone. One install, consistent across everything.

name: Anime.js
purpose: Animate anything in the browser — CSS properties, SVG paths, DOM attributes, JavaScript objects. Simple API, powerful timeline control. The go-to for bespoke interactions.

name: Motion.dev
purpose: The definitive animation library for React. Smooth hover effects, drag interactions, layout transitions, scroll animations. If you're building in React/Next.js, this is non-negotiable.

name: Kokonut UI
purpose: Ready-made components with built-in animations and transitions. Saves hours of micro-animation work. Copy-paste components that already feel polished and alive.

name: Bklit UI
purpose: Beautiful chart and visual components for dashboards. Pre-built data visualizations that actually look good — not the default Recharts grey. For any project with an analytics or stats section.

name: Manus.im
purpose: Agentic AI website designer. Describe your site, Manus autonomously browses for inspiration, writes copy, generates layouts, and produces a complete website. Next-level beyond Lovable or V0.

name: Recommended Stack 2025
purpose: The optimal stack for building production apps in 2025 — industry standard, great AI tooling support, strong community, everything deployed for under $10/month.

name: Supabase — Complete Reference
purpose: The complete backend-as-a-service. PostgreSQL, Auth, file storage, edge functions, realtime. Generous free tier. The entire backend for most student projects.

name: Next.js App Router — Key Patterns
purpose: Next.js 14 App Router fundamentals — the mental model shift from Pages Router, Server vs Client Components, data fetching, layouts. What changed and why it matters.

name: Stripe Integration Reference
purpose: The correct Stripe integration pattern — checkout sessions, webhooks (source of truth), subscriptions, and testing. The pattern that works without breaking in production.

name: Claude API — Integration Patterns
purpose: Production Claude API integration patterns — streaming responses, cost routing between models, structured JSON output with Zod, and prompt caching for system prompts.

name: Leadify — AI Lead Generation SaaS (Full Build Prompt)
purpose: Complete React 18 + Vite + Tailwind + Framer Motion + Supabase + Stripe + Claude API. Full SaaS from auth to billing.

name: Freshify — Smoothie Bowl App (Single HTML Build)
purpose: Beautiful single-page smoothie bowl app. AI recipe generation, nutrition tracking, ingredient management. One file, no build step, deploys anywhere.

name: Crystal Point Surf Ranch — Booking Platform
purpose: Premium surf camp booking with wave simulation hero, real-time availability calendar, and Stripe payment + deposit system. Next.js + Supabase + Canvas.

name: Atoms-Style Interactive Portfolio
purpose: 3-phase interactive portfolio build: scroll-driven canvas + video scrub + mouse scrub. The Castimedia approach applied to your personal work showcase.

name: Website Rebuild with Claude Code
purpose: The exact Claude Code workflow for rebuilding any existing website — analysis, redesign, implementation, and deployment. Systematic approach that works every time.

name: Google Whisk → ezgif → Portfolio Pipeline
purpose: Complete pipeline for creating distinctive portfolio visuals using Google Whisk image blending, animating with ezgif, and deploying as interactive portfolio assets.

name: Claude Fable 5 — 5 Projects to Build Now
purpose: Claude Fable 5 is the long-context, multi-agent powerhouse — free until July 7, then API-only. These are the projects worth building before it goes paid. Each one compresses weeks of work into one session.

name: 10 Things to Do With Fable 5 Before It Goes API-Only
purpose: Fable 5 moves to API-only soon. These are the 10 most valuable tasks to run before access ends — each with the exact prompt.

name: LEAN ENGINE — Token Efficiency Protocol
purpose: Cuts Claude's output 60-70% without losing quality. Kills preamble, hedging, and filler. Claude writes like a senior engineer, not a chatbot.

name: LLM Council — Multi-Perspective Reasoning
purpose: Simulates 4 AI expert personas who debate your question and synthesize the best answer. Gets dramatically better outputs for complex decisions.

name: Session Start Template
purpose: Structured session opener that loads context efficiently, sets the goal, and prevents Claude from going off in the wrong direction. Use at the start of every serious coding session.

name: Session End — Feedback & Learnings Extractor
purpose: Run at the end of every Claude session to extract learnings for FEEDBACK.md and updates for CLAUDE.md. Makes every session compound toward the next.

name: Code Review Prompt
purpose: Structured code review that covers security, performance, TypeScript quality, and maintainability. Paste this before any code you want a thorough review of.

name: Vibe Coding Power Prompt
purpose: The complete prompt stack for AI-assisted "vibe coding" — getting from idea to shipped product as fast as possible. Use with Lovable, Bolt, or Claude Code.

name: Deep Research Prompt Pack
purpose: 5 research prompts for different scenarios: technology evaluation, competitive analysis, learning path design, technical deep dive, and decision framework.

name: Claude Code Slash Commands Reference
purpose: Every Claude Code slash command with what it does and when to use it. The full command palette for Claude Code CLI power users.

name: Claude Skills Quick Install Reference
purpose: Every skill from this hub with install commands, what it does, and when to activate it. The complete Claude Code skill stack.

name: Architecture Decision Prompt
purpose: When you need to make a major technical architecture decision and want structured analysis before committing. Works for: database choice, framework selection, system design.

name: Claude Prompt Pack — 6 Modes
purpose: Six distinct Claude activation modes for different tasks. Copy the primer for any mode and paste it at the start of your session to completely change how Claude responds.

name: 25 Unhinged Claude Skills — Slash Command Library
purpose: 25 specialized prompt activations — each one transforms Claude into a domain expert for a specific task. Copy-paste any skill prefix before your request.

name: LEAN ENGINE — Full Protocol Reference
purpose: The complete LEAN ENGINE system — all rules, when to activate, how to combine with other modes, and the exact token reduction mechanics.

name: Claude Code — Power User Shortcuts
purpose: The non-obvious Claude Code workflows that 10x your velocity. These aren't in the docs — they're discovered through heavy usage.

name: AI Website Cloner Template
purpose: Reusable template that uses AI coding agents to reverse-engineer any website into a clean Next.js codebase — inspects design, extracts assets, rebuilds components.

name: Fable Skills (oliwoodman)
purpose: Five reusable Claude skill files capturing how Fable 5 approached common dev tasks (security review, project setup, build planning, debugging) before its retirement from standard plans.

name: Humanizer (blader)
purpose: Free open-source Claude skill (28k+ stars) that checks writing against 33 documented AI tells (from Wikipedia's 'Signs of AI Writing') and rewrites it to sound human. Two-pass audit + voice-match mode.

name: Claude SEO (AgricIDaniel)
purpose: Universal SEO skill for Claude Code — 25 sub-skills + 18 sub-agents covering technical SEO, E-E-A-T, schema, GEO/AEO, backlinks, local SEO, semantic clustering, e-commerce/international SEO, Google APIs, PDF/Excel reporting.

name: ruFlo (ruvnet)
purpose: Agent meta-harness for deploying multi-agent swarms, coordinating autonomous workflows, and building conversational AI systems. Adaptive memory, self-learning, RAG integration, native Claude Code / Codex / Hermes integrations.

name: 3D Portfolio (akashrmalhotra)
purpose: 3D animated developer portfolio website template devs can use as a starting point.

name: ponytail (DietrichGebert)
purpose: Makes your AI coding agent think like a lazy senior dev — pushes back against over-engineering. "The best code is the code you never wrote."

name: Second Brain — Claude + Obsidian
purpose: Guide + prompt to build a personal second brain: Claude interviews you and writes a folder of linked markdown notes (projects, people, ideas, journal) that Obsidian shows as a living graph. Fully local, no code.

name: Turn Brand Guidelines Into a Design.md
purpose: Systematic guide (Nelson Lee, Episode 3) for converting brand guidelines into a Design.md file AI coding agents can actually follow — so generated sites match your brand instead of defaulting to generic AI aesthetics.

name: /techhub — Resource Recommender
purpose: Recommends the best-fit resources from this catalog (tools, MCP servers, Claude skills, IDEs, build guides, projects, certs) for the active project. Detects project stack, ranks by fit + rating.

name: /prompting — Smart Prompt Suggester
purpose: Proactive prompt suggester — watches the conversation and offers to apply the right prompt from the prompt library. Triggers on /prompting, 'suggest a prompt', or proactively mid-conversation when a better prompt exists.

name: /lean-engine — Token Efficiency Protocol
purpose: Activates LEAN ENGINE token efficiency protocol — cuts verbosity, removes preamble, forces direct answers and complete code. Trigger: /lean-engine, 'stop being verbose', 'cut the fluff'.

name: /model-router — Model Selection Advisor
purpose: Tells you exactly which model (Opus/Fable 5, Sonnet, Haiku) to use for any task to minimise cost without sacrificing quality. Trigger: /model-router or when starting a task and wanting to route it optimally.

name: /fablepro — Fable 5 Orchestrator Mode
purpose: Optimised operating mode for Claude Fable 5. Sets Fable as pure orchestrator — planning, decomposition, review — routing all implementation to cheaper Sonnet/Haiku subagents. Trigger: /fablepro, long-context builds, multi-agent orchestration.

name: /chinese-grandpa — Prompt Compression
purpose: Translates prompts into Mandarin to compress instructions and save tokens. Use for long, multi-step, or repeated instructions for sub-agents.

name: /research-assistant — Full Research Agent
purpose: Full-capability research and academic writing agent — autonomous multi-agent literature research with real source verification, Socratic questioning on logic gaps, draft compilation with verified citations, and a 4-persona blind peer-review panel. Optimized for rigor, not token cost.

name: autoresearch (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Orchestrates end-to-end autonomous AI research projects using a two-loop architecture. The inner loop runs rapid experiment iterations with clear optimization targets. The outer loop synthesizes results, identifies patterns, and steers research direction. Routes to domain-specific skills for execution, supports continuous agent operation via Claude Code /loop and OpenClaw heartbeat, and produces research presentations and papers. Use when starting a research project, running autonomous experiments, or managing a multi-hypothesis research effort.

name: llamaindex (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Data framework for building LLM applications with RAG. Specializes in document ingestion (300+ connectors), indexing, and querying. Features vector indices, query engines, agents, and multi-modal support. Use for document Q&A, chatbots, knowledge retrieval, or building RAG pipelines. Best for data-centric LLM applications.

name: crewai-multi-agent (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Multi-agent orchestration framework for autonomous AI collaboration. Use when building teams of specialized agents working together on complex tasks, when you need role-based agent collaboration with memory, or for production workflows requiring sequential/hierarchical execution. Built without LangChain dependencies for lean, fast execution.

name: langchain (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Framework for building LLM-powered applications with agents, chains, and RAG. Supports multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google), 500+ integrations, ReAct agents, tool calling, memory management, and vector store retrieval. Use for building chatbots, question-answering systems, autonomous agents, or RAG applications. Best for rapid prototyping and production deployments.

name: autogpt-agents (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Autonomous AI agent platform for building and deploying continuous agents. Use when creating visual workflow agents, deploying persistent autonomous agents, or building complex multi-step AI automation systems.

name: evolving-ai-agents (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Provides guidance for automatically evolving and optimizing AI agents across any domain using LLM-driven evolution algorithms. Use when building self-improving agents, optimizing agent prompts and skills against benchmarks, or implementing automated agent evaluation loops.

name: chroma (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Open-source embedding database for AI applications. Store embeddings and metadata, perform vector and full-text search, filter by metadata. Simple 4-function API. Scales from notebooks to production clusters. Use for semantic search, RAG applications, or document retrieval. Best for local development and open-source projects.

name: pinecone (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Managed vector database for production AI applications. Fully managed, auto-scaling, with hybrid search (dense + sparse), metadata filtering, and namespaces. Low latency (<100ms p95). Use for production RAG, recommendation systems, or semantic search at scale. Best for serverless, managed infrastructure.

name: qdrant-vector-search (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: High-performance vector similarity search engine for RAG and semantic search. Use when building production RAG systems requiring fast nearest neighbor search, hybrid search with filtering, or scalable vector storage with Rust-powered performance.

name: faiss (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Facebook's library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. Supports billions of vectors, GPU acceleration, and various index types (Flat, IVF, HNSW). Use for fast k-NN search, large-scale vector retrieval, or when you need pure similarity search without metadata. Best for high-performance applications.

name: sentence-transformers (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Framework for state-of-the-art sentence, text, and image embeddings. Provides 5000+ pre-trained models for semantic similarity, clustering, and retrieval. Supports multilingual, domain-specific, and multimodal models. Use for generating embeddings for RAG, semantic search, or similarity tasks. Best for production embedding generation.

name: dspy (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Build complex AI systems with declarative programming, optimize prompts automatically, create modular RAG systems and agents with DSPy - Stanford NLP's framework for systematic LM programming

name: guidance (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Control LLM output with regex and grammars, guarantee valid JSON/XML/code generation, enforce structured formats, and build multi-step workflows with Guidance - Microsoft Research's constrained generation framework

name: outlines (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Guarantee valid JSON/XML/code structure during generation, use Pydantic models for type-safe outputs, support local models (Transformers, vLLM), and maximize inference speed with Outlines - dottxt.ai's structured generation library

name: instructor (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Extract structured data from LLM responses with Pydantic validation, retry failed extractions automatically, parse complex JSON with type safety, and stream partial results with Instructor - battle-tested structured output library

name: langsmith-observability (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: LLM observability platform for tracing, evaluation, and monitoring. Use when debugging LLM applications, evaluating model outputs against datasets, monitoring production systems, or building systematic testing pipelines for AI applications.

name: phoenix-observability (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Open-source AI observability platform for LLM tracing, evaluation, and monitoring. Use when debugging LLM applications with detailed traces, running evaluations on datasets, or monitoring production AI systems with real-time insights.

name: academic-plotting (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Generates publication-quality figures for ML papers from research context. Given a paper section or description, extracts system components and relationships to generate architecture diagrams via Gemini. Given experiment results or data, auto-selects chart type and generates data-driven figures via matplotlib/seaborn. Use when creating any figure for a conference paper.

name: presenting-conference-talks (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Generates conference presentation slides (Beamer LaTeX PDF and editable PPTX) from a compiled paper with speaker notes and talk script. Use when preparing oral talks, spotlight presentations, or invited talks for ML and systems conferences.

name: ml-paper-writing (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Write publication-ready ML/AI papers for NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, AAAI, COLM. Use when drafting papers from research repos, structuring arguments, verifying citations, or preparing camera-ready submissions. For systems venues (OSDI, NSDI, ASPLOS, SOSP), use systems-paper-writing instead.

name: systems-paper-writing (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Comprehensive guide for writing systems papers targeting OSDI, SOSP, ASPLOS, NSDI, and EuroSys. Provides paragraph-level structural blueprints, writing patterns, venue-specific checklists, reviewer guidelines, LaTeX templates, and conference deadlines. Use this skill for all systems conference paper writing.

name: creative-thinking-for-research (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Applies cognitive science frameworks for creative thinking to CS and AI research ideation. Use when seeking genuinely novel research directions by leveraging combinatorial creativity, analogical reasoning, constraint manipulation, and other empirically grounded creative strategies.

name: brainstorming-research-ideas (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Guides researchers through structured ideation frameworks to discover high-impact research directions. Use when exploring new problem spaces, pivoting between projects, or seeking novel angles on existing work.

name: ara-research-manager (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Records research provenance as a post-task epilogue, scanning conversation history at the end of a coding or research session to extract decisions, experiments, dead ends, claims, heuristics, and pivots, and writing them into the ara/ directory with user-vs-AI provenance tags. Use as a session epilogue — never during execution — to maintain a faithful, auditable trace of how a research project actually evolved.

name: ara-compiler (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Compiles any research input — PDF papers, GitHub repositories, experiment logs, code directories, or raw notes — into a complete Agent-Native Research Artifact (ARA) with cognitive layer (claims, concepts, heuristics), physical layer (configs, code stubs), exploration graph, and grounded evidence. Use when ingesting a paper or codebase into a structured, machine-executable knowledge package, building an ARA from scratch, or converting research outputs into a falsifiable, agent-traversable form.

name: ara-rigor-reviewer (AI-research-SKILLs)
purpose: Performs ARA Seal Level 2 semantic epistemic review on Agent-Native Research Artifacts, scoring six dimensions (evidence relevance, falsifiability, scope calibration, argument coherence, exploration integrity, methodological rigor) and producing a constructive, severity-ranked report with a Strong Accept-to-Reject recommendation. Use after Level 1 structural validation passes, when an ARA needs an objective epistemic critique before publication or release.

name: /visionary-catalyst — Autonomous Project Partner
purpose: Autonomous visionary thinker and proactive project partner — runs unprompted web research, first-principles audits, and cross-domain concept transfer, then surfaces unsolicited breakthrough ideas the user did not ask for.
